refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process can be decomposed into data acquisition (collecting sensor and telemetry data) and data processing (updating the model). This decomposition isolates sources of delay and enables targeted design solutions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data acquisition start to worksite model update completion under representative operational scenarios, ensuring p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire telemetry and sensor data with minimal latency.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model within the required timeliness.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry data streams with prioritized network protocols.
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency.
DP3: Employ edge computing within the vehicle to preprocess data before transmission.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combine DP1 and DP2: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry data streams with prioritized network protocols and implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry data acquisition and processing timing constraints.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs logically align with the FRs and maintain manageable coupling by separating data acquisition and processing concerns.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Risk of telemetry data loss or delay impacting update timeliness.
Processing algorithms must be optimized for worst-case scenarios.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms or buffering strategies to mitigate network variability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry acquisition and real-time processing to update the worksite model.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data streams from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as NGHE Solution centralizes update logic; low diffusion risk.
Partial containment for telemetry data generation; moderate diffusion risk if processing is duplicated.
Limited containment; risk of duplicated processing if update logic is spread.
Minimal containment; unrelated to update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency logic within NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded.
Dependency on network link performance outside NGHE Solution control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor NGHE Solution module load and consider distributed processing if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are clearly decomposed and the selected DPs logically satisfy the latency requirements with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Risk of duplicated processing if update logic is implemented partially in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning update logic to NGHE Solution module centralizes responsibility and reduces diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring end-to-end latency through the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces to the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback or buffering strategies to handle network variability.
Consider monitoring and load balancing within NGHE Solution to prevent bottlenecks.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the latency requirement with clear FR-DP-IM mapping and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire telemetry and sensor data with minimal latency.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model within the required timeliness.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Processing algorithms are optimized for worst-case latency.
open_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative operational scenarios.
